questions.question: A hospital has $12$ liters of a $20%$ saline solution. How much pure saline should be added to make a $30%$ solution?
A patient is prescribed $250$ milligrams of a medication that is $25%$ effective. What is the amount of effective medication?
A virus is growing at a rate of $20%$ per hour. If there are initially $100$ cells, how many will there be after $2$ hours?
A doctor orders $500$ milliliters of a $10%$ dextrose solution. How much dextrose should be added to make a $20%$ solution?
A medical trial has a $15%$ success rate. If $120$ patients participate, how many are expected to respond to treatment?
A pharmacist mixes $300$ milligrams of a $50%$ concentration medication with $200$ milligrams of a $25%$ concentration medication. What is the concentration of the resulting mixture?
A patient's white blood cell count increases by $15%$ in one day. If the initial count was $8,000$ cells per microliter, what is the new count?
A researcher is studying the effect of a new medication on $50$ patients. The medication has a $30%$ success rate. How many patients are expected to show improvement?
A solution of $80%$ acid is mixed with a solution of $20%$ acid. If the resulting mixture has a $50%$ acid concentration, what is the ratio of the $80%$ solution to the $20%$ solution?

A doctor prescribes a medication with a $40%$ concentration of the active ingredient. If the patient needs $30$ milligrams of the active ingredient, how much of the medication should be administered? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Read each question carefully before answering.
Use a calculator if necessary, but be sure to show your work.
Check your units and make sure they are consistent.
